title: 1. Introduction
text: cleaningkit package built automate process survey data cleaning validation. workflow package divided two primary phases: Validation Cleaning Process Applying Cleaning Logs Produce Final Output

title: 1. Introduction
text: initial phase involves: Running suite validation functions (starting validate_). functions performs specific check dataset generates corresponding log flagged issues. running individual checks, combine generated logs single, comprehensive cleaning log also includes dataset facilitate review process. Concurrently, extract, prepare, create specialized cleaning log handling “” text responses. detailed instructions examples running functions, please see Validation Cleaning Process article.

title: 1. Introduction
text: cleaning logs (including “” text responses log) manually reviewed filled , proceed application phase. phase involves: Reading reviewed cleaning logs back R. Evaluating logs ensure match raw data correctly. Applying specified cleaning actions dataset. Reviewing cleaned data verify required cleaning steps successfully implemented. Creating final consolidated output workbook. final output contains README, raw data, newly cleaned data, applied cleaning logs one place. details step, please see Applying Cleaning Logs article.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: package provides suite validate_* functions run various data quality checks. function returns list containing checked dataset log flagged issues. validate_duration(): Checks survey duration falls within acceptable time range. validate_completeness(): Flags surveys fewer specified number answered questions. validate_refused(): Identifies surveys excessive number “Refused” answers. validate_logical_with_list(): Runs batch logical checks defined external checklist dataset. validate_interview_time(): Flags interviews conducted implausible times day (e.g., middle night). validate_duplicates(): Detects suspiciously similar surveys (soft duplicates) based differing column counts. validate_duplicate_questions(): Flags specific questions enumerator repeatedly recorded exact answer across multiple surveys. validate_country_of_interview(): Flags cases interview country matches respondent’s nationality journey start. validate_back_to_back(): Flags interviews conducted enumerator suspiciously short negative gaps . quick look can load data run validation checks:
code: library(cleaningkit)  # Load your raw data and tool survey schema raw_data <- read_raw_data("path/to/data.xlsx", tool_survey = survey_sheet)  # Run validations (each step passes the result to the next) checked_data <- raw_data |>   # 1. Survey duration makes sense (e.g. between 15 and 60 minutes)   validate_duration(column_to_check = "_duration", lower_bound = 15, upper_bound = 60) |>   # 2. Minimum of 100 answered questions   validate_completeness(min_content_cells = 100) |>   # 3. Maximum of 6 "Refused" answers   validate_refused(max_refused = 6) |>   # 4. Interviews conducted at a plausible time (e.g. between 5am and 10pm)   validate_interview_time(earliest_hour = 5, latest_hour = 22) |>   # 5. Suspiciously similar responses (soft duplicates differing in <= 7 columns)   validate_duplicates(tool_survey = survey_sheet, threshold = 7) |>   # 6. Duplicated answers for specific questions by the same enumerator   validate_duplicate_questions(questions_to_check = c("Q161_1", "Q162_1")) |>   # 7. Country of interview shouldn't match nationality or journey start   validate_country_of_interview() |>   # 8. Implausible back-to-back interviews (gap < 10 minutes)   validate_back_to_back(threshold_mins = 10) |>   # 9. External logical checks (requires a checklist dataframe)   validate_logical_with_list(list_of_check = logical_list,     check_id_column = "check_id",     check_to_perform_column = "check_to_perform",     columns_to_clean_column = "columns_to_clean",     description_column = "description")  # Look at the issues logged for any of the checks print(checked_data$duration_log) print(checked_data$back_to_back_log)

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)

code: #---------------------------------- # combine logs #----------------------------------  # Since all logs are stored in the `checked_data` list,  # you can pass it directly to create a single combined log: combined_log <- cleaningkit::create_combined_log(   list_of_log = checked_data,   dataset_name = "checked_dataset" )  #---------------------------------- # create cleaning log #----------------------------------  cleaningkit::create_cleaning_log(   write_list = combined_log,   output_path = paste0(     "path/to/output/",     Sys.Date(),     "_follow-ups.xlsx"   ) )

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: can extract prepare “” responses dataset using two functions: prepare_other_responses(): Combines responses sheets single dataframe ready use save_other_responses(). can optionally pass character vector specific question names questions parameter want process subset questions. save_other_responses(): function saves prepared responses Excel workbook specific formatting data validation.
code: # Prepare the other responses dataframe other_responses_df <- prepare_other_responses(   raw_data = raw_data,   other_db = other_db,   tool_choices = choices_sheet )  # Save to an Excel workbook for review save_other_responses(   df = other_responses_df,   save_location = "output" )

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: cleaning logs responses reviewed edited, can apply changes produce final clean dataset. read_cleaning_log(): Reads edited cleaning log Excel file removes duplicates. evaluate_cleaning_log(): Evaluates cleaning log correctness, ensuring UUIDs actions match raw data. apply_cleaning_log(): Applies actions cleaning log directly raw dataset produce clean dataset. read_other_responses(): Reads processes edited “” responses Excel file. apply_other_responses(): Integrates cleaned “” responses dataset. combine_reviewed_logs(): Merges main cleaning log -responses log single comprehensive log. review_cleaned_data(): Compares cleaned dataset log verify changes successfully applied. export_final_output(): Creates final Excel workbook containing raw data, clean data, comprehensive cleaning log.
code: #---------------------------------- # apply cleaning #---------------------------------- cleaning_log <- read_cleaning_log(path = "path/to/log.xlsx", raw_dataset = raw_data) evaluated_log <- evaluate_cleaning_log(cleaning_log = cleaning_log, raw_dataset = raw_data) cleaned_data_list <- apply_cleaning_log(raw_dataset = raw_data, cleaning_log = evaluated_log$cleaning_log)  other_log <- read_other_responses(path = "path/to/other.xlsx", dataset = raw_data, other_db = other_db, tool_choices = choices_sheet) cleaned_data_list <- apply_other_responses(dataset = cleaned_data_list$clean_dataset, other_log = other_log)  #---------------------------------- # combine cleaning logs #---------------------------------- final_log <- combine_reviewed_logs(main_log = evaluated_log$cleaning_log, other_log = other_log, dataset = raw_data)  #---------------------------------- # review clean dataset #---------------------------------- review_log <- review_cleaned_data(raw_dataset = raw_data, clean_dataset = cleaned_data_list$dataset, cleaning_log = final_log)  #---------------------------------- # create final workbook #---------------------------------- export_final_output(   raw_dataset = raw_data,    clean_dataset = cleaned_data_list$dataset,    combined_log = final_log,    output_path = "output/final_output.xlsx" )

title: Apply a Validated Cleaning Log to the Raw Dataset — apply_cleaning_log
text: raw_dataset dataframe — original raw data cleaned. cleaning_log dataframe containing validated cleaning log, returned read_cleaning_log() checked evaluate_cleaning_log(). Must remaining issues evaluation log passed . raw_uuid_column Name uuid column raw_dataset. Default "_uuid". log_uuid_col Name uuid column cleaning_log. Default "Survey UUID". log_question_col Name question/column column cleaning_log. Default "Question number". log_new_value_col Name new-value column cleaning_log. Default "New value". log_action_col Name action-type column cleaning_log. Default "Action taken". change_response_values Character vector action values mean "change cell new_value". Default covers three map change_response: c("recoded", "addition", ""). blank_response_values Character vector action values mean "blank cell (set NA)". Default "delete_data_point". remove_survey_values Character vector action values mean "remove entire survey". Default "discard". no_action_values Character vector action values skip entirely. Default "no_action". skip_label_row Logical. TRUE (default), first row raw_dataset preserved ONA label/description row never modified cleaning log. Changes applied rows 2+. restore_types Logical. TRUE (default), type.convert() called cleaned dataset restore numeric logical column types coerced character. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), summary message printed completion.

title: Apply a Validated Cleaning Log to the Raw Dataset — apply_cleaning_log
text: list two elements: clean_dataset cleaned dataframe. audit_log dataframe recording every change applied, columns uuid, question, action, value_before, value_after.

title: Apply a Validated Cleaning Log to the Raw Dataset — apply_cleaning_log
text: Action types : recoded Sets cell [uuid, question] new_value. Maps change_response. addition Sets cell [uuid, question] new_value (filling previously empty cell). Maps change_response. Sets cell [uuid, question] new_value. Maps change_response. delete_data_point Sets cell [uuid, question] NA. Maps blank_response. discard Removes entire survey (row) uuid dataset. Maps remove_survey. no_action Row skipped; change made. Column-wide changes: uuid "all_data" (case- insensitive), change applied every row question rather one specific survey. mirrors original behaviour bulk corrections. Audit trail: returned list includes audit_log dataframe showing every cell-level change made (uuid, question, action, old value actually dataset, new value written). separate cleaning log passed reflects actually done. Type restoration: changes applied, type.convert used restore numeric, logical, integer columns coerced character cleaning process.
